themeWords.meaning: Judge; the name of Jacob’s son, his tribe, and their territory/city
A carved idol; an image fashioned from wood or stone

sentenceTheme: The tribe of Dan, seeking an inheritance, steals Micah’s idols and priest, conquers Laish, and establishes idolatrous worship in Dan.

verses.text: In those days there was no king in Israel: and in those days the tribe of the Danites sought them an inheritance to dwell in; for unto that day all their inheritance had not fallen unto them among the tribes of Israel.

highlightedWords.meaning: Israel; the covenant name for God’s people and nation
Inheritance; allotted portion or possession

verses.text: And the children of Dan sent of their family five men from their coasts, men of valour, from Zorah, and from Eshtaol, to spy out the land, and to search it; and they said unto them, Go, search the land: who when they came to mount Ephraim, to the house of Micah, they lodged there.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judge; the name of Jacob’s son, his tribe, and their territory/city
Land; earth, territory, ground

verses.text: When they were by the house of Micah, they knew the voice of the young man the Levite: and they turned in thither, and said unto him, Who brought thee hither? and what makest thou in this place? and what hast thou here?

highlightedWords.meaning: House; dwelling, household, temple
Levite; descendant of Levi, member of the Levitical tribe

verses.text: And he said unto them, Thus and thus dealeth Micah with me, and hath hired me, and I am his priest.

highlightedWords.meaning: Micah; personal name meaning ‘Who is like Yah?’
Priest; one who officiates in religious rites

verses.text: And they said unto him, Ask counsel, we pray thee, of God, that we may know whether our way which we go shall be prosperous.

highlightedWords.meaning: God; deity, the true God, sometimes gods
Way; road, path, manner of life

verses.text: And the priest said unto them, Go in peace: before the LORD is your way wherein ye go.

highlightedWords.meaning: Priest; one who officiates in religious rites
The personal covenant name of God (Yahweh)

verses.text: Then the five men departed, and came to Laish, and saw the people that were therein, how they dwelt careless, after the manner of the Zidonians, quiet and secure; and there was no magistrate in the land, that might put them to shame in any thing; and they were far from the Zidonians, and had no business with any man.

highlightedWords.meaning: Zidonians; inhabitants of Zidon/Sidon
Land; earth, territory, ground

verses.text: And they came unto their brethren to Zorah and Eshtaol: and their brethren said unto them, What say ye?

highlightedWords.meaning: Brother; kinsman, relative (plural ‘brethren’)
To say; speak, declare

verses.text: And they said, Arise, that we may go up against them: for we have seen the land, and, behold, it is very good: and are ye still? be not slothful to go, and to enter to possess the land.

highlightedWords.meaning: Land; earth, territory, ground
To possess, inherit, dispossess, take possession

verses.text: When ye go, ye shall come unto a people secure, and to a large land: for God hath given it into your hands; a place where there is no want of any thing that is in the earth.

highlightedWords.meaning: God; deity, the true God, sometimes gods
Hand; power, control

verses.text: And there went from thence of the family of the Danites, out of Zorah and out of Eshtaol, six hundred men appointed with weapons of war.

highlightedWords.meaning: Article; implement, vessel, tool; weapon
War; battle, combat

verses.text: And they went up, and pitched in Kirjathjearim, in Judah: wherefore they called that place Mahanehdan unto this day: behold, it is behind Kirjathjearim.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judah; the southern tribe/kingdom, also a personal name
Place; location, site

verses.text: And they passed thence unto mount Ephraim, and came unto the house of Micah.

highlightedWords.meaning: Ephraim; a tribe of Israel and its hill-country region
House; dwelling, household, temple

verses.text: Then answered the five men that went to spy out the country of Laish, and said unto their brethren, Do ye know that there is in these houses an ephod, and teraphim, and a graven image, and a molten image? now therefore consider what ye have to do.

highlightedWords.meaning: Ephod; priestly garment or cultic object
Household gods; idols used for divination

verses.text: And they turned thitherward, and came to the house of the young man the Levite, even unto the house of Micah, and saluted him.

highlightedWords.meaning: Levite; descendant of Levi, member of the Levitical tribe
House; dwelling, household, temple

verses.text: And the six hundred men appointed with their weapons of war, which were of the children of Dan, stood by the entering of the gate.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judge; the name of Jacob’s son, his tribe, and their territory/city
Gate; city gate, entrance

verses.text: And the five men that went to spy out the land went up, and came in thither, and took the graven image, and the ephod, and the teraphim, and the molten image: and the priest stood in the entering of the gate with the six hundred men that were appointed with weapons of war.

highlightedWords.meaning: A carved idol; an image fashioned from wood or stone
Ephod; priestly garment or cultic object

verses.text: And these went into Micah's house, and fetched the graven image, the ephod, and the teraphim, and the molten image. Then said the priest unto them, What do ye?

highlightedWords.meaning: House; dwelling, household, temple
A carved idol; an image fashioned from wood or stone

verses.text: And they said unto him, Hold thy peace, lay thine hand upon thy mouth, and go with us, and be to us a father and a priest: is it better for thee to be a priest unto the house of one man, or that thou be a priest unto a tribe and a family in Israel?

highlightedWords.meaning: Priest; one who officiates in religious rites
Israel; the covenant name for God’s people and nation

verses.text: And the priest's heart was glad, and he took the ephod, and the teraphim, and the graven image, and went in the midst of the people.

highlightedWords.meaning: Priest; one who officiates in religious rites
Household gods; idols used for divination

verses.text: So they turned and departed, and put the little ones and the cattle and the carriage before them.

highlightedWords.meaning: Little ones; children, dependents
Livestock; cattle, possessions

verses.text: And when they were a good way from the house of Micah, the men that were in the houses near to Micah's house were gathered together, and overtook the children of Dan.

highlightedWords.meaning: House; dwelling, household, temple
Judge; the name of Jacob’s son, his tribe, and their territory/city

verses.text: And they cried unto the children of Dan. And they turned their faces, and said unto Micah, What aileth thee, that thou comest with such a company?

highlightedWords.meaning: To call out, cry, proclaim
Judge; the name of Jacob’s son, his tribe, and their territory/city

verses.text: And he said, Ye have taken away my gods which I made, and the priest, and ye are gone away: and what have I more? and what is this that ye say unto me, What aileth thee?

highlightedWords.meaning: God; deity, the true God, sometimes gods
Priest; one who officiates in religious rites

verses.text: And the children of Dan said unto him, Let not thy voice be heard among us, lest angry fellows run upon thee, and thou lose thy life, with the lives of thy household.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judge; the name of Jacob’s son, his tribe, and their territory/city
Soul; life, person, living being

verses.text: And the children of Dan went their way: and when Micah saw that they were too strong for him, he turned and went back unto his house.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judge; the name of Jacob’s son, his tribe, and their territory/city
House; dwelling, household, temple

verses.text: And they took the things which Micah had made, and the priest which he had, and came unto Laish, unto a people that were at quiet and secure: and they smote them with the edge of the sword, and burnt the city with fire.

highlightedWords.meaning: Laish; a northern city later called Dan
Sword; dagger, destructive weapon

verses.text: And there was no deliverer, because it was far from Zidon, and they had no business with any man; and it was in the valley that lieth by Beth-rehob. And they built a city, and dwelt therein.

highlightedWords.meaning: Sidon/Zidon; Phoenician coastal city
City; town, urban center

verses.text: And they called the name of the city Dan, after the name of Dan their father, who was born unto Israel: howbeit the name of the city was Laish at the first.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judge; the name of Jacob’s son, his tribe, and their territory/city
Israel; the covenant name for God’s people and nation

verses.text: And the children of Dan set up the graven image: and Jonathan, the son of Gershom, the son of Manasseh, he and his sons were priests to the tribe of Dan until the day of the captivity of the land.

highlightedWords.meaning: Judge; the name of Jacob’s son, his tribe, and their territory/city
A carved idol; an image fashioned from wood or stone

verses.text: And they set them up Micah's graven image, which he made, all the time that the house of God was in Shiloh.

highlightedWords.meaning: Shiloh; Israelite sanctuary town in Ephraim
God; deity, the true God, sometimes gods
